channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Detecting Web Server Probing & Fuzzing in Traefik with Automated Cloudflare Response
item.description: This article shows how a customized Elastic Security ES|QL detection rule can identify web server probing and fuzzing activity in Traefik logs and automatically block the attacking IP via Cloudflare.

item.title: Worries About AI’s Risks to Humanity Loom Over the Trial Pitting Musk Against OpenAI’s Leaders
item.description: Musk said that he could have founded OpenAI as a for-profit company, just like the other companies he started or took over. “I deliberately chose this,” he said, “for the public good.” The post Worries About AI’s Risk...

item.title: ICYMI: April 2026 @AWS Security
item.description: Read all about the latest AWS security features, compliance updates, and hands-on resources in our new, monthly digest posts. You’ll find expert blog posts, new service capabilities, code samples, and workshops. AWS S...

item.title: New PCPJack worm steals credentials, cleans TeamPCP infections
item.description: A new malware framework called PCPJack is stealing credentials from exposed cloud infrastructure while actively removing TeamPCP's access to the systems. [...]

item.title: U.S. CISA adds a flaw in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a flaw in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security A...

item.title: Australia warns of ClickFix attacks pushing Vidar Stealer malware
item.description: The Australian Cyber Security Center (ACSC) is warning organizations of an ongoing malware campaign using the ClickFix social engineering technique to distribute the Vidar Stealer info-stealing malware. [...]

item.title: Unplug your way to better code
item.description: Cybersecurity concepts — logs, packets, DNS exfiltration, and more — are usually intangible, and its practitioners are prone to mental fatigue, Amy takes a second to yell at you to go touch grass.

item.title: Ivanti EPMM CVE-2026-6973 RCE Under Active Exploitation Grants Admin-Level Access
item.description: Ivanti is warning that a new security flaw impacting Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) has been explored in limited attacks in the wild. The high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-6973 (CVSS score: 7.2), is a case of impr...

item.title: What Mozilla learned running an AI security bug hunting pipeline on Firefox
item.description: Over the past several months, Mozilla ran an agentic harness powered by Claude Mythos Preview across Firefox’s source code, identifying 271 security bugs that were fixed in Firefox 150, with additional fixes shipped i...

item.title: $250 million cryptocurrency heist funded luxury fashion, nightclub parties, and private jets
item.description: 20-year-old California resident Marlon Ferro, known online as “GothFerrari,” was sentenced to 78 months in prison for his role in a cryptocurrency theft operation tied to more than $250 million in stolen digital asset...

item.title: Vendor Says Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack Contained
item.description: The software developer has identified the impacted systems, removed potentially compromised files, and validated installation packages. The post Vendor Says Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack Contained appeared first on...

item.title: Fake Claude AI Site Drops Beagle Backdoor on Windows Users
item.description: Sophos finds fake Claude site spreading DonutLoader and a new Beagle backdoor via DLL sideloading

item.title: One keypress is all it takes to compromise four AI coding tools
item.description: Developers clone unfamiliar repositories all the time. Open-source projects, work from teammates, sample code from a tutorial, a library someone recommended on a forum. The convention is old and reasonable: you look a...

item.title: State-sponsored hackers likely behind zero-day attacks on Palo Alto firewalls
item.description: Palo Alto Networks believes the in-the-wild exploitation of a zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-0300) in its firewalls is likely the work of state-sponsored threat actors. A flaw with no patch (yet) CVE-2026-0300 is a...

item.title: Cisco Patches High-Severity Vulnerabilities in Enterprise Products
item.description: Successful exploitation of the flaws could lead to code execution, server-side request forgery attacks, and denial-of-service conditions. The post Cisco Patches High-Severity Vulnerabilities in Enterprise Products app...

item.title: Node.js 26 ships with Temporal API enabled by default
item.description: Developers managing JavaScript runtimes have a new major version to evaluate. Node.js 26.0.0 brings the long-awaited Temporal API to the platform alongside an updated V8 engine, a refreshed HTTP client, and several lo...

item.title: Facial recognition arrives at the gates of Disney’s magic kingdom
item.description: Disney has equipped select entrance lanes at Disneyland Park and Disney California Adventure Park with facial recognition technology, saying the system is intended to streamline re-entry procedures and help prevent fr...

item.title: CallPhantom Android scam reached 7.3 million downloads on Google Play
item.description: Scams targeting Android users in India and across the Asia-Pacific region have grown around a long-standing curiosity gap: the desire to look up call records tied to a phone number. A cluster of 28 fraudulent apps on...

item.title: Kloudfuse 4.0 delivers AI-governed observability and scalable workload isolation
item.description: Kloudfuse has announced the general availability of Kloudfuse 4.0. The release helps enterprises meet rising compliance requirements, adopt AI-driven observability with production-grade governance, and scale their obs...

item.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux adds post-quantum security and AI-driven automation in latest releases
item.description: Red Hat has announced the upcoming general availability of Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.2 and 9.8. Building on the innovation of Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10, the latest versions help address security threats, speed AI...

item.title: AWS achieves SNI 27017, SNI 27018, and SNI 9001 certifications for the AWS Asia Pacific (Jakarta) Region
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) achieved three Standar Nasional Indonesia (SNI) certifications for the AWS Asia Pacific (Jakarta) Region: SNI ISO/IEC 27017:2015, SNI ISO/IEC 27018:2019, and SNI ISO 9001:2015. SNI represents...

item.title: Boost Security Raises $4 Million for SDLC Defense Platform
item.description: The company is expanding its platform’s capabilities with the acquisition of SecureIQx and Korbit.ai. The post Boost Security Raises $4 Million for SDLC Defense Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Legacy Security Tools Are Failing Data Protection, Capital One Software Report Finds
item.description: Traditional network security tools are undermining data protection, with Forrester and Capital One Software research warning AI adoption is impossible without rethinking data security

item.title: Claude Code OAuth Tokens Can Be Stolen Through Stealthy MCP Hijacking
item.description: Mitiga researchers say attackers can silently redirect Claude Code MCP traffic, intercept OAuth tokens, and maintain persistent access to connected SaaS platforms. The post Claude Code OAuth Tokens Can Be Stolen Throu...

item.title: Cline Kanban Flaw Lets Websites Hijack AI Coding Agents
item.description: Oasis Security finds critical Cline kanban WebSocket flaw exposing AI coding agents to hijack

item.title: Chrome 148 Rolls Out With 127 Security Fixes
item.description: The fresh browser update resolves critical-severity integer overflow and use-after-free vulnerabilities. The post Chrome 148 Rolls Out With 127 Security Fixes appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: The Browser Is Breaking Your DLP: How Data Slips Past Modern Controls
item.description: Your security controls aren't failing, they're missing where most of today's work actually happens. Keep Aware shows how browser activity like copy/paste and AI prompts bypass traditional protections. [...]

item.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
item.description: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient Zero" infection. In 20...

item.title: OpenAI and Anthropic LLMs Used in Critical Infrastructure Cyber-Attack, Warns Dragos
item.description: Commercial AI models were used to help plan and conduct cyber-attack against operational technology of a water and drainage facility, say researchers

item.title: Attackers Could Exploit AI Vision Models Using Imperceptible Image Changes
item.description: Cisco’s AI security researchers have analyzed ways to target vision-language models (VLMs) using pixel-level perturbation. The post Attackers Could Exploit AI Vision Models Using Imperceptible Image Changes appeared f...

item.title: Americans sentenced for running 'laptop farms' for North Korea
item.description: Two U.S. nationals were sentenced to 18 months in prison each for operating so-called laptop farms that helped North Korean IT workers fraudulently obtain remote employment at nearly 70 American companies. [...]

item.title: Why Security in 2026 Requires Continuous Threat and Exposure Management (CTEM) at Scale
item.description: Let's be honest, the patching window just shrank to something no practitioner or organization can keep up with. Organizations now need to operate in an environment that must assume breach, which means fundamentals lik...

item.title: How Cloudflare responded to the “Copy Fail” Linux vulnerability
item.description: When a critical Linux kernel privilege escalation was publicly disclosed, Cloudflare's security and engineering teams detected, investigated, and mitigated the threat across our global fleet, confirming zero customer...

item.title: AI Coding Agents Could Fuel Next Supply Chain Crisis
item.description: “TrustFall” attack shows how AI coding agents can be manipulated into launching stealthy supply chain compromises. The post AI Coding Agents Could Fuel Next Supply Chain Crisis appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: World's First AI-Driven Cyberattack Couldn't Breach OT Systems
item.description: The most sophisticated AI-integrated campaign to date hit a brick wall in the form of a SCADA login screen.

item.title: Webinar: Why modern attacks require both security and recovery
item.description: Modern attacks don't stop at initial compromise. This webinar explores why security and recovery must work together to reduce downtime and improve resilience. [...]

item.title: Webinar Today: Securing Identity Across Humans, Machines and AI
item.description: From service accounts to AI-driven processes, identity is evolving faster than most security programs can adapt. Discover strategies for reducing risk and regaining control. The post Webinar Today: Securing Identity A...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Edge Plaintext Passwords, ICS 0-Days, Patch-or-Die Alerts and 25+ New Stories
item.description: Bad week. Turns out the easiest way to get hacked in 2026 is still the same old garbage: shady packages, fake apps, forgotten DNS junk, scam ads, and stolen logins getting dumped into Discord channels like it’s normal...

item.title: Day Zero Readiness: The Operational Gaps That Break Incident Response
item.description: Having an incident response retainer, or even a pre-approved external incident response firm, is not the same as being ready for an incident. A retainer means someone will answer the phone. Operational readiness deter...

item.title: From Android TVs to routers: the xlabs_v1 Mirai-based botnet built for DDoS attacks
item.description: A new Mirai‑based botnet, xlabs_v1, hijacks ADB‑exposed IoT devices for powerful DDoS attacks, with 21 flooding methods and DDoS‑for‑hire use. A new Mirai‑derived botnet called xlabs_v1 is hijacking internet‑exposed d...

item.title: Daemon Tools Developer Confirms Software Was Trojanized
item.description: A China-linked threat actor backdoored a version of Daemon Tools to infect thousands

item.title: PyPI Packages Deliver ZiChatBot Malware via Zulip APIs on Windows and Linux
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered three packages on the Python Package Index (PyPI) repository that are designed to stealthily deliver a previously unknown malware family called ZiChatBot on Windows and Linux...

item.title: Researchers Spot Uptick in Use of Vercel for Phishing Campaigns
item.description: Cofense has warned of a “significant” increase in phishing campaigns abusing Vercel platform

item.title: Claude AI Guided Hackers Toward OT Assets During Water Utility Intrusion
item.description: Dragos has published a report describing how threat actors used Claude AI in an attack on a water and drainage utility in Mexico. The post Claude AI Guided Hackers Toward OT Assets During Water Utility Intrusion appea...

item.title: Open-source MCP server monitoring for Python apps
item.description: Pythonic Model Context Protocol servers handle tool calls, session events, module imports, and subprocess activity. BlueRock has released MCP Python Hooks, an open source runtime sensor that gives developers a way to...

item.title: vm2 Node.js Library Vulnerabilities Enable Sandbox Escape and Arbitrary Code Execution
item.description: A dozen critical security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in the vm2 Node.js library that could be exploited by bad actors to break out of the sandbox and execute arbitrary code on susceptible systems. vm2 is an o...

item.title: Multi-model AI is creating a routing headache for enterprises
item.description: Application teams are moving AI inference into production systems that support business operations. Enterprises are expanding traffic management, identity controls, observability, and routing systems for multiple AI m...

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 7th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9922, (Thu, May 7th)
An Adaptive Cyber Analytics UI for Web Honeypot Logs [Guest Diary], (Wed, May 6th)
item.description: [This is a Guest Diary by Eric Roldan, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu BACS program]

item.title: TCLBANKER: Brazilian Banking Trojan Spreading via WhatsApp and Outlook
item.description: REF3076 uses a trojanized Logitech installer to deploy TCLBANKER, a Brazilian banking trojan with environment-gated payloads, WPF fraud overlays, and self-propagating WhatsApp and Outlook worm modules.

item.title: MuddyWater hackers use Chaos ransomware as a decoy in attacks
item.description: The MuddyWater Iranian hackers disguised their operations as a Chaos ransomware attack, relying on Microsoft Teams social engineering to gain access and establish persistence. [...]

item.title: Iran-Linked APT Posed as Chaos Ransomware Member in Espionage Campaign
item.description: Rapid7 reveals an Iranian false flag operation masquerading as a Chaos ransomware attack

item.title: Apache fixes critical HTTP/2 double-free flaw CVE-2026-23918 enabling RCE
item.description: Apache fixed several flaws in HTTP Server, including CVE-2026-23918 (CVSS score of 8.8), a double-free bug in HTTP/2 that could allow remote code execution. The Apache Software Foundation has released updates to fix m...

item.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
item.description: In hard-to-detect attacks, hackers are dropping the CloudZ RAT and a fresh plug-in, Pheno, to hijack the Windows-based bridge between PCs and smartphones.

item.title: Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS flaw exploited for remote code execution
item.description: Palo Alto Networks warns of a critical PAN-OS flaw (CVE-2026-0300) that is under active attack, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. Palo Alto Networks has warned that a critical PAN-OS vulnerability, track...

item.title: CrowdStrike Named a Leader in the First-Ever Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cyberthreat Intelligence Technologies
Teams calls are about to get a lot harder to fake
item.description: Microsoft Teams Calling is getting a new feature that will warn users about suspicious inbound VoIP calls from first-time external callers who might be impersonating trusted brands. The post Teams calls are about to g...

item.title: Sysdig delivers cloud security that runs inside AI coding agents
item.description: Sysdig announced headless cloud security, a cyberdefense platform designed for the agentic AI era. Sysdig Headless Cloud Security enables customers to drop the traditional, one-size-fits-all UI approach and equip thei...

item.title: Taiwan High-Speed Rail Emergency Braking Hack: How a Student Stopped the Trains and Exposed a Major Security Gap
item.description: Taiwan high‑speed rail was disrupted after a 23‑year‑old student spoofed signals and triggered an emergency alarm, stopping four trains for nearly an hour. Taiwan high‑speed rail system, one of the most important piec...

item.title: Hackers abuse Google ads for GoDaddy ManageWP login phishing
item.description: A phishing campaign delivered through Google sponsored search results is targeting credentials for ManageWP, GoDaddy's platform for managing fleets of WordPress websites. [...]

item.title: Yet Another Way to Bypass Google Chrome's Encryption Protection
item.description: Authors of the VoidStealer Trojan uncovered a way to get around Google's App-Bound Encryption (ABE), opening the door to infostealers.

item.title: Instructure Breach Exposes Schools' Vendor Dependence
item.description: ShinyHunters' attack on Instructure, which owns the widely used Canvas learning management system (LMS), carries big questions about the trust educational institutions put into their vendors.

item.title: New compliance guide available: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 on AWS
item.description: We have released our latest compliance guide, ISO/IEC 42001:2023 on AWS, which provides practical guidance for organizations designing and operating an Artificial Intelligence Management System (AIMS) using AWS servic...

item.title: Critical vm2 sandbox bug lets attackers execute code on hosts
item.description: A critical vulnerability in the popular Node.js sandboxing library vm2 allows escaping the sandbox and executing arbitrary code on the host system. [...]

item.title: Arctic Wolf kicks 250 employees out of the pack to save money for AI
item.description: Cuts appear to hit sales, product, and marketing, accounting for under 10% of staff

item.title: New Cisco DoS flaw requires manual reboot to revive devices
item.description: Cisco patched a Crosswork Network Controller and Network Services Orchestrator denial-of-service vulnerability that requires manually rebooting targeted systems for recovery. [...]

item.title: 1 in 8 employees totally cool with selling work credentials
item.description: 13% say they’ve sold logins or know someone who has, survey suggests

item.title: DAEMON Tools devs confirm breach, release malware-free version
item.description: Disc Soft Limited, the maker of DAEMON Tools Lite, confirmed that the software had been trojanized in a supply chain attack and released a new, malware-free version. [...]

item.title: The Jenkins Threat Landscape
item.description: What usage patterns, plugin adoption, and configuration choices reveal about the Jenkins attack surface.

item.title: Why ransomware attacks succeed even when backups exist
item.description: Backups don't fail because they're missing, they fail because attackers destroy them first. Acronis explains how ransomware targets backup systems before encryption, leaving no path to recovery. [...]

item.title: LABScon25 Replay | Please Connect to the Foreign Entity to Enhance Your User Experience
item.description: Joe FitzPatrick reveals how consumer imports of networked devices pose a real security risk to small businesses and critical infrastructure alike.

item.title: Webinar: Why network incidents escalate and how to fix response gaps
item.description: Most network incidents don't escalate due to a lack of alerts; they escalate when response breaks down. This webinar explores how to fix gaps in triage, enrichment, and coordination. [...]

item.title: The Hacker News Launches 'Cybersecurity Stars Awards 2026' — Submissions Now Open
item.description: For nearly 20 years, we at The Hacker News have mostly told scary stories about cyberspace — big hacks, broken systems, and new threats. But behind every headline, there’s a quieter, better story. It’s the story of le...

item.title: From Stuxnet to ChatGPT: 20 News Events That Shaped Cyber
item.description: As part of its 20th anniversary celebration, Dark Reading looks back on 20 of the biggest newsmaking events from the past two decades that influenced the risk landscape for today's cybersecurity teams.

item.title: Romanian Man Extradited to US for Role in Hacking Scheme 17 Years Ago
item.description: Gavril Sandu, 53, was indicted in 2017, but was arrested and extradited to the United States only in 2026. The post Romanian Man Extradited to US for Role in Hacking Scheme 17 Years Ago appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Your AI Agents Are Already Inside the Perimeter. Do You Know What They're Doing?
item.description: Analysts recently confirmed what identity security teams have quietly feared: AI agents are being deployed faster than enterprises can govern them. In their inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, Gartner states t...

item.title: Intel 471 speeds threat hunting and remediation with Retroactive Threat Detections
item.description: Intel 471 has announced Retroactive Threat Detections (RTD), a new capability within its Verity471 platform. RTD helps security teams quickly understand the impact of new threats on their environments. This transforms...

item.title: Rowhammer Attack Against NVIDIA Chips
item.description: A new rowhammer attack gives complete control of NVIDIA CPUs. On Thursday, two research teams, working independently of each other, demonstrated attacks against two cards from Nvidia’s Ampere generation that take GPU...

item.title: Extreme Networks introduces Agent ONE for autonomous enterprise networking
item.description: Extreme Networks has introduced Extreme Agent ONE, a new class of AI agents for enterprise networking. Moving beyond generic, prompt-based AI, Extreme Agent ONE runs on the Extreme AI stack purpose-built for enterpris...

item.title: UiPath adds agentic AI capabilities to Automation Suite for government agencies
item.description: UiPath has announced the release of agentic AI capabilities on UiPath Automation Suite. The Automation Suite updates help government agencies and regulated industries accelerate agentic AI and automation adoption and...

item.title: CISA: Critical Infrastructure Must Master Isolation, Recovery
item.description: The agency has issued guidance to help critical infrastructure operators prepare for cyberattacks by foreign threat actors. The post CISA: Critical Infrastructure Must Master Isolation, Recovery appeared first on Secu...

item.title: 8×8 updates CX platform with AI, analytics, and frontline management capabilities
item.description: 8×8 has released a set of platform updates to the 8×8 Platform for CX that target the operational gaps most commonly stalling organizations, including AI deployments requiring months of integration, queues IT teams ca...

item.title: Proton Mail brings quantum-safe email encryption to all accounts
item.description: Post-quantum protection is now available as an optional feature in Proton Mail across all plans, including the free tier. How post-quantum protection works Once enabled, Proton Mail generates new encryption keys desig...

item.title: Insights into the clustering and reuse of phone numbers in scam emails
item.description: Talos has recently started to collect and gather intelligence around phone numbers within emails as an additional indicator of compromise (IOC). In this blog, we discuss new insights into in-the-wild phone number reus...

item.title: New Relic advances AI observability with new intelligence layer
item.description: New Relic has announced New Relic Knowledge, a new platform capability that integrates telemetry and knowledge sources to enhance issue detection and resolution. By combining real-time telemetry with historical incide...

item.title: Sophisticated Quasar Linux RAT Targets Software Developers
item.description: The persistent, evasive implant provides remote access, surveillance, and credential exfiltration capabilities. The post Sophisticated Quasar Linux RAT Targets Software Developers appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: groundcover expands its observability platform with enhanced Synthetic Monitoring and RUM
item.description: groundcover has expanded its capabilities with new and enhanced offerings across Synthetic Monitoring and Real User Monitoring (RUM). These innovations give engineering teams greater visibility into the user experienc...

item.title: ServiceNow strengthens enterprise AI security with Autonomous Security & Risk platform
item.description: ServiceNow has launched Autonomous Security & Risk to govern every AI agent, identity, and connected asset. Armis delivers continuous asset intelligence across code, IT, OT, IoT, and connected assets, while Veza provi...

item.title: Google's Android Apps Get Public Verification to Stop Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Google has announced expanded Binary Transparency for Android as a way to safeguard the ecosystem from supply chain attacks. "This new public ledger ensures the Google apps on your device are exactly what we intended...

item.title: Megaport enhances network resilience with integrated DDoS protection
item.description: Megaport has announced the launch of Megaport DDoS Protection. This new built-in security capability for Megaport Internet allows customers to filter malicious traffic directly within the Megaport network, rather than...

item.title: One in Eight Workers Has Sold Their Corporate Logins
item.description: Cifas says that 13% of employees admit selling company credentials to a former colleague

item.title: India orders infosec red alert in case Mythos sparks crime spree
item.description: Securities regulator urges market players to develop new strategies and nail cyber-basics before AI models fuel mass attacks

item.title: Malicious PyTorch Lightning update hits AI supply chain security
item.description: A malicious PyTorch Lightning update (v2.6.3) on PyPI spread briefly, stealing credentials and raising major concerns about AI supply chain security. A malicious update of the PyTorch Lightning library exposed develop...

item.title: Middle East Cyber Battle Field Broadens — Especially in UAE
item.description: As the war with Iran continues, breach attempts targeting the United Arab Emirates tripled in a few weeks — many targeting critical infrastructure.

item.title: Welcome to headless cloud security
item.description: From the start, we built Sysdig around a simple belief: security should reflect how people and systems actually work. That’s why we focused on runtime. That’s why we built in the open. We’ve always aligned with where...

item.title: Trellix Source Code Breach Highlights Growing Supply Chain Threats
item.description: Info is scant, but such breaches can reveal where a security product's controls are located and how detections are designed, giving attackers a leg up.

item.title: UAT-8302 and its box full of malware
item.description: Cisco Talos is disclosing UAT-8302, a sophisticated, China-nexus advanced persistent threat (APT) group targeting government entities in South America since at least late 2024 and government agencies in southeastern E...

item.title: OWASP Foundation Unveils Its Strategic Plan for a World Without Insecure Software
item.description: The OWASP Foundation is entering a defining moment. This strategic plan outlines how OWASP will move from being a recognized voice in security to a truly transformative force in the industry. Inside this document, you...

item.title: Vimeo confirms breach via third-party vendor impacts 119K users
item.description: Hackers stole data of 119,000 Vimeo users in April. The breach, linked to a third‑party vendor, exposed personal details. Vimeo confirmed a data breach after the ShinyHunters gang stole personal information of 119,000...

item.title: ScarCruft Hacks Gaming Platform to Deploy BirdCall Malware on Android and Windows
item.description: The North Korea-aligned state-sponsored hacking group known as ScarCruft has compromised a video game platform in a supply chain espionage attack, trojanizing its components with a backdoor called BirdCallto likely ta...

item.title: Instructure hacker claims data theft from 8,800 schools, universities
item.description: The hacker behind a breach at education technology giant Instructure claims to have stolen 280 million data records for students and staff from 8,809 colleges, school districts, and online education platforms. [...]

item.title: U.S. court sentences Karakurt ransomware negotiator to 8.5 years
item.description: Deniss Zolotarjovs was sentenced to 8.5 years in the U.S. after pleading guilty to money laundering and fraud tied to ransomware. Deniss Zolotarjovs, a Latvian national linked to the Karakurt ransomware gang, has been...

item.title: Research Hub Bridges Cybersecurity Gap for Under-Resourced Organizations
item.description: The UC Berkeley Center for Long-Term Cybersecurity (CLTC) offers tools and support to schools, local governments, and non-profits as they defend themselves against a growing volume of cyberattacks.

item.title: Why Security Leadership Makes or Breaks a Pen Test
item.description: Well-run security drills go beyond checking audit boxes to identify and address trouble spots. Effective leaders can ensure proper scope, access, and follow-through, but it’s not easy.

item.title: Critical Android vulnerability CVE-2026-0073 fixed by Google
item.description: Google patched a critical Android flaw (CVE‑2026‑0073) that lets attackers run code remotely without user action. Google released a security update for Android to address a critical remote code execution flaw, tracked...

item.title: ServiceNow clears agents for landing with new AI control tower
item.description: ServiceNow acquisitions Veza and Traceloop join to monitor agents and AI workflows

item.title: Attackers are cashing in on fresh 'CopyFail' Linux flaw
item.description: Researchers dropped a reliable root exploit and it didn’t sit idle for long

item.title: Real estate giant confirms vishing incident as ShinyHunters and Qilin both come knocking
item.description: Cushman & Wakefield activated incident response protocols after serial extortionists issued separate threats

item.title: Romance scammers turn sweet talk into £102M payday
item.description: Victims losing £280K a day to fake profiles and sob stories

item.title: Singapore boffins get diverse SIEMs singing in harmony with agentic rule translation
item.description: Vendors all use different formats. This tech translates them all so you can smooth your SOC

item.title: Introducing AI traffic analysis dashboards for AWS WAF
item.description: As AI agents, bots, and programmatic access become an increasingly significant portion of web traffic, organizations need better tools to understand, analyze, and manage this activity. Today, we’re excited to announce...

item.title: Vulnerability Summary for the Week of April 27, 2026
item.description: High Vulnerabilities Primary Vendor -- Product Description Published CVSS Score Source Info Patch Info n/a-- OVMS3 3.3.005 Buffer overflow vulnerability in Open Vehicle Monitoring System 3 (OVMS3) 3.3.005. In canforma...

item.title: The EOL Blind Spot in Your CVE Feed: What SCA Tools Miss
item.description: Critical vulnerabilities can exist in open source software your scanners don't check. HeroDevs reveals how EOL software creates blind spots in CVE feeds and SCA tools, and how you can receive a free end-of-life scan f...

item.title: Tanium Atlas aims to accelerate threat response in the AI era
item.description: Tanium announced Tanium Atlas, an autonomous operating system (OS) that gives a single IT or security operator the data, guidance and reach to accomplish what once required an entire team – moving from intent to outco...

item.title: Unpatched flaws turn Ollama’s auto-updater into a persistent RCE vector, researchers say
item.description: Researchers at Striga have disclosed two vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-42248, CVE-2026-42249) in Ollama’s Windows auto-updater that, when chained together, may allow an attacker to covertly plant a persistent executable t...

item.title: LastPass Mobile Smart Scanner improves password security
item.description: LastPass has launched Mobile Smart Scanner, a solution that converts photographs of typed or handwritten credentials into structured, ready-to-use password entries that can be reviewed, saved, and autofilled directly...

item.title: Five ways to use Kiro and Amazon Q to strengthen your security posture
item.description: A Monday morning security alert flags unauthorized access attempts, security group misconfigurations, and AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy violations. Your team needs answers fast. Security teams are us...

item.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
item.description: A proof-of-concept exploit (PoC) shows how someone with admin privileges can exploit the issue to steal passwords, and thus use them to engage in further malicious activity.

item.title: Hacker Conversations: Joey Melo on Hacking AI
item.description: AI red team specialist details his methods for manipulating AI guardrails through jailbreaking and data poisoning, helping developers harden machine learning models. The post Hacker Conversations: Joey Melo on Hacking...

item.title: Introducing Penetration Test Findings: Unified Offensive Security in Wiz
item.description: Streamline pen-testing by unifying findings from bug bounties, manual audits, and Wiz Red Agent into a single, context-rich view.
